completionCheck.description: When true (default), inject the [[TASK_COMPLETE]] / [[TASK_BLOCKED]] instructions and resume the session until a marker appears (stall guard applies). When false ('simple mode'), send the prompt verbatim and mark the task done after the first run that exits OK; only a usage limit triggers a resume. Overridable per task via tasks[].completionCheck.

maxStalls.description: Only used when completionCheck is true. After this many consecutive OK runs that repeat the same response without a completion marker, the task fails with a 'no progress' reason.

model.description: Model name(s) passed through to the CLI. A single string, OR an array of strings in preference order (Task 6): when a run hits a usage limit the runner switches to the next model immediately, and only falls back to waiting for a reset once every listed model is limit-exhausted. Examples — claude: opus, sonnet, haiku, claude-opus-4-8, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5; codex: gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.3-codex-spark; gemini: gemini-3.1-pro-preview, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3.5-flash, gemini-3.1-flash-lite, gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash; agy (Antigravity CLI) / copilot (GitHub Copilot CLI): run 'agy models' or 'copilot models' to list what your account can use — passed through as '--model'. Copilot also supports session naming/resume, JSONL output, and reasoning-effort flags. Local Ollama models (e.g. qwen3.5:9b, nemotron-3-nano:4b) are also accepted; pair them with extraArgs ["--oss", "--local-provider", "ollama"] to run locally. A local claude task requires a model (it is passed to 'ollama launch --model').

effort.description: Reasoning effort. Allowed values depend on cli (see the per-cli rules below). Use null or omit the field when the CLI/model does not support effort. Copilot supports low, medium, high, xhigh, and max; it also accepts --effort / --reasoning-effort in its CLI.

completionCheck.description: Per-task override of settings.completionCheck. true keeps the [[TASK_COMPLETE]] marker workflow; false sends this task's prompt verbatim and marks it done after the first OK run (only a usage limit resumes it).

extraArgs.description: Extra CLI flags (array form is safest). Permission flags go here (claude --permission-mode acceptEdits, codex --sandbox workspace-write, gemini --approval-mode auto_edit, agy --dangerously-skip-permissions, copilot --allow-tool=read,write,shell(npm:*),shell(npx:*),shell(git:*), copilot --deny-tool=shell(git push), copilot --no-ask-user, or full automation with copilot --allow-all --no-ask-user). To run a local Ollama model, include --oss --local-provider ollama: codex passes these through natively, while claude is rewritten as 'ollama launch claude --model <model> --yes -- <claude args>'. (agy/copilot have no Ollama path.)

effort.description: Gemini CLI has no effort flag. Must be null (or omitted). For Gemini 3+ models use thinkingLevel, and for Gemini 2.5 models use thinkingBudget, via settings or generation config — not via this field.
Antigravity CLI (agy) has no --effort flag. Must be null (or omitted).

GitHub Copilot CLI --effort / --reasoning-effort accepts low, medium, high, xhigh, max. The runner passes this through to the CLI along with --model, --name / --resume, -p, --output-format=json, and --no-ask-user.

Claude Code --effort accepts low, medium, high, xhigh, max. 'ultracode' exists only in the interactive /effort menu and is rejected by the --effort flag, so it is not allowed here. If a model does not support the requested level (e.g. xhigh on Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6), Claude Code falls back to the highest supported level at or below it.
Claude Haiku 4.5 does not support effort. Must be null (or omitted).
Codex model_reasoning_effort accepts minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh ('none' is plan-mode only and not valid for exec runs). xhigh availability is model-dependent.
